name: Almadenes to Almorchón — circular via PR MU-25
name_original: Almadenes a Almorchón — circular por PR MU-25
region: Zepa Sierra Del Molino, Embalse Del Quípar Y Llanos Del Cagitán
region_intro: Jogging in Zepa Sierra Del Molino, Embalse Del Quípar Y Llanos Del Cagitán offers diverse terrain across its 283.49 km² area. The region features extensive plains and gentle hills, providing open routes for various running levels. For more challenging runs, the northern and eastern boundaries include steep mountains with pine forests and sub-shrub and esparto scrubland. The Quípar reservoir and riverbank forests along the Segura River also offer scenic paths. Best jogging routes in Zepa Sierra Del Molino, Embalse Del Quípar Y Llanos Del Cagitán The most popular jogging route is Cañón de Almadenes Loop, a 7.3 miles (11.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 23 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the scenic Cañón de Almadenes. Another top favourite among local runners is Almadenes to Almorchón — circular via PR MU-25, a moderate 8.2 miles (13.2 km) path. This route winds through varied landscapes including the Cañón de Almadenes area. Local runners also love the Running loop from Calasparra, a 4.0 miles (6.4 km) trail leading through the area around Calasparra, often completed in about 44 minutes. Jogging in Zepa Sierra Del Molino, Embalse Del Quípar Y Llanos Del Cagitán is defined by plains, gentle hills, and pine forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging mountain trails. The routes in Zepa Sierra Del Molino, Embalse Del Quípar Y Llanos Del Cagitán are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 1000 runners have used komoot to explore Zepa Sierra Del Molino, Embalse Del Quípar Y Llanos Del Cagitán's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Zepa Sierra Del Molino, Embalse Del Quípar Y Llanos Del Cagitán?
A: There are over 80 running routes documented in the region, catering to various fitness levels. These include easy loops, moderate trails, and more challenging mountain runs.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when jogging in this area?
A: The region offers a diverse landscape for runners. You'll find extensive plains and gentle hills, ideal for easier runs, alongside more abrupt and steep mountains with pine forests and scrubland for those seeking a greater challenge. The area also features picturesque routes along the Quípar reservoir and shaded paths by the Segura River.

Q: Are there any easy running routes suitable for beginners or a relaxed jog?
A: Yes, there are several easy options. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1374405567/cueva-de-la-serreta-circular-desde-el-parking-de-panel-informativo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva De La Serreta — circular desde el parking de panel informativo&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.6 km (2.2 miles) route, perfect for a gentle run. The region's plains and gentle hills also provide many accessible paths.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I might see while running?
A: The area is rich in natural beauty. You can encounter impressive sights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1345858" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alfonso XIII Reservoir Dam and Quípar River Canyon&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5321575" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of the Cañón de Almadenes&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5412052" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Argos Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. These features add scenic appeal to your run.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the Zepa Sierra Del Molino area?
A: Absolutely. Many routes are designed as loops, offering a convenient way to start and finish at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375637119/almadenes-to-almorchon-circular-via-pr-mu-25" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almadenes to Almorchón — circular via PR MU-25&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 13.2 km (8.2 miles) path that winds through varied landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Zepa Sierra Del Molino, Embalse Del Quípar Y Llanos Del Cagitán?
A: Given the semi-arid climate of the Murcia region, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for jogging. During warmer periods, the shaded paths along the Segura River can be particularly inviting. Winters are mild, making it possible to run year-round, though summers can be very hot.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running trails?
A: Yes, the region's extensive plains and gentle hills provide many suitable options for families. Routes with less elevation gain and shorter distances, such as the easy trails around Calasparra, are great for a family outing. Always check the route details for distance and difficulty to ensure it matches your family's needs.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running routes?
A: While specific dog rules for every trail are not detailed, the region's commitment to natural conservation suggests that dogs are generally welcome on trails, often requiring them to be on a leash. It's always advisable to check local signage or specific route information for any restrictions before heading out with your canine companion.

Q: What do other runners say about the routes in this region?
A: The running routes in Zepa Sierra Del Molino, Embalse Del Quípar Y Llanos Del Cagitán are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 20 reviews. Runners often praise the varied terrain, from open plains to scenic river paths, and the opportunity to connect with nature.

Q: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants near the jogging routes?
A: The ZEPA encompasses parts of several municipalities, including Calasparra, Cieza, and Mula. These towns offer various amenities such as cafes, restaurants, and accommodation where you can refuel or relax after your run. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31464648" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Calasparra&lt;/a&gt;, for example, starts and ends near facilities in the town.

Q: Where can I find parking for the running trails?
A: Many trailheads, especially for popular routes, will have designated parking areas. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1374405567/cueva-de-la-serreta-circular-desde-el-parking-de-panel-informativo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva De La Serreta — circular desde el parking de panel informativo&lt;/a&gt; explicitly mentions starting from a parking lot. It's recommended to check the specific route details on komoot for parking information before your visit.
tips: This is a very open area with many nice trails. For a short stretch, PR-MU 25 and GR 7 are the same trail. Make sure you grab the right one when they split.
The Almadenes Canyon Interpretation Center is a place that promotes different tourist activities in the surrounding environment. It has a restaurant with local food.

waypoints.name: Junction of PR-MU 25 and GR 7 Trails
